Victory Day, observed every year on May 9, commemorates the Soviet Union’s victory over Nazi Germany in 1945 and remains one of the most significant state ceremonies in #Russia.

The Soviet Union bore the brunt of the war against Nazi Germany, suffering an estimated 27 million military and civilian deaths during what is known in Russia as the Great Patriotic War.

This year's commemoration saw the Red Square parade featuring marching formations from Russian military academies, strategic missile forces personnel, aerospace forces units, and naval crews.

Russian aerobatic teams also flew over #Moscow, while Su-25 attack aircraft concluded the aerial display by trailing the colors of the Russian flag across the sky.

As customary, Russia's President Vladimir #Putin congratulated the people of Russia on a defiant note, declaring, "I am firmly convinced that our cause is just, we are together, and victory has always been and will always be ours. Glory to the victorious nation, glory to the veterans, glory to the Armed Forces of Russia. Happy holiday to you. Happy Victory Day."

#RussiaVictoryDay #GreatPatrioticWar
